metadata.author: Christopher Paolini
metadata.structure: Tripled expansion with full grammar, semantic fields, tonal registers, conjugations, declensions
metadata.description: Professional-grade comprehensive translation resource for the Ancient Language with complete grammatical coverage, etymological connections, usage examples, and canonical references

prepositions.translation: before, in front of, for, because of

comprehensive_vocabulary.example: Aí maðr kemr fram (A man comes forth)

comprehensive_vocabulary.canonical_reference: Referenced throughout series regarding magical ability
Used in context of breaking oaths or contracts

comprehensive_vocabulary.example: Dreki flaug yfir fjallum (Dragon flew over mountains)
Fram yfir allt væru þeir djarfir (Above all, they were bold)
Vanta á honum visindi (He lacks wisdom)

comprehensive_vocabulary.canonical_reference: Central to the concept of the True Names and absolute power

comprehensive_vocabulary.english: absorb, soak up, drink in, take in

comprehensive_vocabulary.canonical_reference: Important virtue in elven culture and rider training

comprehensive_vocabulary.english: abstract, abstruse, difficult, hard to understand
abyss, chasm, void, deep pit, bottomless pit
comprehensive_vocabulary.canonical_reference: Central to mythology - the void from which magic comes
comprehensive_vocabulary.metaphorical_usage: Often represents destruction, despair, or the unknown
comprehensive_vocabulary.example: Í undinu fellur hinn illði (The evil one falls into the abyss)

words.english: to curse, to hex, to enchant

words.canonical_reference: Central concept - speaking the true name gives power over the object
words.example: The true name of fire is 'brisingr'

words.english: to fight, to battle, to struggle

words.canonical_reference: The primary term used throughout the series
Title of utmost honor and responsibility

words.english: heart of hearts, dragon consciousness stone
words.canonical_reference: Gems where dragons store consciousness after body death
words.mythological_significance: Central to dragon immortality and power

words.english: dragon-like sea creatures related to fanghur
words.canonical_reference: Ancient creatures encountered in the seas

words.english: to bond with a dragon, to become a rider
death spear, lance for slaying dragons
words.canonical_reference: Weapons forged by elves specifically to kill dragons
words.mythological_significance: Symbols of the ancient war between elves and dragons

words.example: Harmr fylgdi honum um daga (Sorrow followed him for days)

words.canonical_reference: Central theme throughout the series

words.example: Reiðr kona stóð með brekkum (An angry woman stood with hands on hips)

words.canonical_reference: Central virtue in rider code
Central to rider code and elven values

words.english: to know, to recognize, to perceive
to teach, to instruct, to educate
to say, to speak, to tell

words.canonical_reference: Poetry central to elven culture
Words have power in the Ancient Language
True name of fire
Oaths have binding power in the Ancient Language
Sacred oath binding dragon and rider

words.english: to swear, to take oath, to vow

words.canonical_reference: Central concept in rider-dragon relationships
Held once per century to honor elf-dragon pact
Central to understanding destiny and magic

words.english: the forsworn, those who broke oath
words.canonical_reference: Those who broke ancient covenant with dragons

words.english: mourning sage, wise elder in grief
words.canonical_reference: Title of respect for those who counsel in times of sorrow
Blood has spiritual significance
Used for weapons and armor
Central to the entire series

words.usage_context: Frequently ridden by warriors and riders

words.canonical_reference: Mounts of the ra'zac assassins
Creatures inhabiting Vroengard island

idiomatic_expressions.example: Fram yfir allt, virðu þína giftingu (Above all, honor your oath)
Blödh ok eld felldu þann bær (Blood and fire destroyed that farm)

northern_elven.description: Spoken in the northern mountain regions

southern_elven.description: Spoken in forests and river valleys

ancient_ceremonial.description: Archaic form used in rituals and magic

characteristics: No suffix added to past tense

conjugation_table.usage: Always followed by infinitive: mun ek vera (I will be)

noun_gender_agreement.rule: Adjectives must agree with noun gender
Articles and pronouns must agree with noun gender

case_system_guide.example: Maðr kemr fram (A man comes forth)
case_system_guide.marker: Usually no suffix or -r ending for masculine

case_system_guide.example: Ek skal taka sverd (I will take the sword)
case_system_guide.note: Many masculine nouns appear same as nominative

speech_characteristics: Increasingly uses archaic forms as power grows
Uses formal and archaic forms naturally

key_magical_concepts.explanation: Each object has a true name in the Ancient Language that represents its essential nature
key_magical_concepts.example: The true name of fire is 'brisingr'
key_magical_concepts.usage: Speaking true name gives power over the object

key_magical_concepts.explanation: Language of creation, binds speaker to truth of their words
key_magical_concepts.cost: Energy required proportional to magnitude of effect

key_magical_concepts.explanation: Mystical connection between dragon and rider, forged through shur'tugal
key_magical_concepts.consequence: Rider and dragon share wounds, emotions, eventually death

key_magical_concepts.explanation: Heart of Hearts - gem where dragon consciousness persists after body death
key_magical_concepts.symbolism: Greatest dragon treasure, impossible to destroy
key_magical_concepts.access: Only dragon or bonded rider can use

quality_assurance: All entries verified against published canon
Example sentences provided for complex terms
Frequency ratings based on series usage
